summary: No-ship: the slice breaks the promised opaque/verbatim context contract and adds an unsafe markdown link renderer in the shared chat surface.

findings.title: Web channel mutates caller-supplied `context`/`mode` instead of forwarding them verbatim
findings.recommendation: Stop decoding/parsing `context` and stop trimming `mode` in the channel path. Treat both as opaque values from the fitting and forward them unchanged; if URL transport requires encoding, decode only the transport layer and preserve the original value exactly. Also remove the extra `mode.trim()` normalization in `buildGatewayChatBody` so the gateway body matches the caller input byte-for-byte when present.

findings.title: Shared `ClaudeChat` markdown renderer now emits unsanitized links, enabling attacker-controlled script/navigation payloads
findings.recommendation: Do not interpolate `href` directly into HTML. Restrict allowed schemes to a safe allowlist (`http`, `https`, and the translated `garrison` form), escape attribute values, and reject `javascript:`/`data:`/other active-content URLs before passing them to `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Because this hook is global, verify the fix against existing dev-env `ClaudeChat` usage too.
